details: New SIEM correlation rules deployed in January. 12 incidents detected in Q1.
Improved runbook automation reduced triage time.
3 critical vulnerabilities identified and remediated within 72 hours.
16 of 17 high vulns remediated on time. 1 delayed due to trading platform deployment freeze.
350 employees. 340 completed. 10 pending (new hires in onboarding grace period).
3 campaigns. Lowest click rate in engineering (2.8%). Highest in sales (8.2% — still within target).
UEBA detected insider threat INC-2025-019 within 2 hours of anomalous access.
INC-2025-019 contained within 4 hours. Investigation took additional 2 weeks due to legal involvement.
5 critical vulnerabilities. All remediated within 72 hours.
24 of 25 high vulns on time. Improvement from Q1.
341 completed of 350 eligible.
New targeted training for sales team reduced their click rate to 5.9%.
Best quarter for detection. SIEM + UEBA correlation achieving sub-2-hour detection.
INC-2025-041 (supply chain) detected and blocked in CI/CD within 1 hour.
4 critical vulnerabilities. All within SLA.
29 of 30 high vulns. Consistent improvement trend.
New hire onboarding process improvements driving higher completion.
All departments now below 6%. Lowest annual rate.
INC-2025-057 (exec phishing) reported by CFO within 3 minutes.
Fastest average MTTR. Automation improvements in triage.
2 critical vulns. Both patched within 48 hours.
Automated patching for non-critical workloads contributing to improvement.
Consistent high completion. Holiday period did not impact.
Slight uptick from Q3 attributed to more sophisticated simulation scenarios.
